methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $85M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Wardstone is a Seed-stage space and aerospace company founded by Sebastian Fischer and Tobias Fischer that has raised $5M to date. The valuation universe comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs, though narrow-stage and valuation-tier filtering within Space & Aerospace yielded insufficient datapoints for robust peer-median benchmarking.

Of three triangulation methods assessed, only Stage-Progression proved applicable. This method uses a $85M proxy based on total raised and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-Series A progression, yielding an implied valuation of $212M. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of disclosed revenue for the target, though the Space & Aerospace sector median P/S multiple stands at 25.8x across the 5 public comparables. Peer-Median methodology was excluded because the narrow peer set—filtered by stage and valuation tier—contained fewer than 3 datapoints.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $181M to $244M, centered at $212M. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue data preventing public-market triangulation, the limited private peer set at this stage and capital tier, and reliance on a single applicable methodology. Any refinement would require either disclosed revenue figures or additional Seed-stage Space & Aerospace comparables with known valuations.
